title: Mastering the Sliding Window Pattern
content: When I first started tackling array and string problems, I always defaulted to nested loops. It worked, but it was incredibly slow. Then I discovered the sliding window pattern. It's essentially a way to optimize O(n^2) brute force solutions down to O(n) by maintaining a 'window' that expands and contracts as you iterate through the data. It's been a total game changer for optimizing my solutions and thinking more efficiently about contiguous subarrays.
